C1: The AI Scientist generates complete scientific papers at a cost of less than $15 per paper, achieving automated reviewing accuracy of 65% on a balanced dataset of ICLR 2022 OpenReview submissions.
C2: SciAgents proposes a bio-inspired composite material integrating silk fibroin with dandelion-derived pigments to achieve a target tensile strength of 1.5 GPa, reducing energy consumption by 30%.
C3: PaperQA outperforms GPT-4 on the closed-book PubMedQA benchmark by 28.4 percentage points, achieving 86.3% accuracy compared to GPT-4's 57.9%, while producing a 0% hallucination rate for citations.
C4: ChemCrow integrates 13 expert-designed tools using a Thought-Action-Observation workflow, resulting in an average human evaluation accuracy score of 9.25, compared to 4.79 for GPT-4.
C5: Toolformer outperforms the 175B parameter GPT-3 on the zero-shot LAMA SQUAD benchmark, achieving a score of 33.8% compared to GPT-3's 26.8% by self-supervising API calls that reduce next-token cross-entropy loss.
C6: ReAct outperforms imitation and reinforcement learning baseline methods by an absolute success rate of 34% on the ALFWorld text-based game and 10% on the WebShop navigation benchmark.
C7: Reflexion agents utilize verbal self-reflection and episodic memory to achieve a 91.0% pass@1 accuracy on the HumanEval Python coding benchmark, outperforming the GPT-4 baseline of 80.1%.
C8: Frontier models evaluated on the MLGYM-Bench framework demonstrate Level 1 Baseline Improvement capabilities, with OpenAI o1-preview achieving the highest Best Attempt AUP@4 score of 1.150 across 13 tasks.
C9: Galactica's 120B parameter model achieves a score of 68.2% on zero-shot LaTeX equation knowledge probes, outperforming the latest GPT-3's score of 49.0% after pre-training on 106 billion scientific tokens.
C10: BioGPT establishes a new state-of-the-art on the PubMedQA question answering benchmark with an accuracy of 78.2%, and achieves an F1 score of 44.98% on the BC5CDR end-to-end relation extraction task.
